2606.29254.title: Travel-Oriented Reasoning Large Language Model via Domain-Specific Knowledge Graphs
2606.29254.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate broad reasoning abilities but struggle with accuracy and reliability in specialized domains such as travel, where reasoning depends on precise definitions, rules, and expert-defined conceptual frameworks, and where confident but unfounded outputs arise from a reasoning failure in which the model has not internalized the underlying domain graph rather than from missing domain knowledge alone. We propose a modular pipeline for building a travel-domain reasoning LLM grounded in an expert-designed knowledge graph (KG). Our pipeline integrates a travel KG that encodes domain entities and their relationships, a bottom-up construction procedure that walks the KG to produce multi-hop question answer (QA) pairs, a supervised fine-tuning stage that embeds the domain knowledge into a reasoning-capable LLM using the generated QA pairs as auditable reasoning traces, and a travel-domain benchmark dataset that measures the fine-tuned model's accuracy and calibration. We evaluate our approach using Qwen3-4B with LoRA adaptation. Our reasoning model achieves an $82.4\%$ exact match on the benchmark. This performance significantly outperforms the pretrained Qwen3-4B baseline at $22.4\%$. A calibration analysis decomposes the residual $17.57\%$ of errors into two distinct failure modes: an over-confident multi-label decoder that predicts both correct answers plus one spurious option on most dual-answer mistakes, and a smaller reasoning failure on single-answer questions where the supporting facts are present in the KG but the model fails to reconstruct the correct multi-hop path. This split confirms that explicit KG-grounded reasoning substantially improves the accuracy and uncertainty interpretation of LLMs in specialized domains, and isolates per-option calibration and trace-length-aware decoding as the next axes of improvement.
2606.29254.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）展現出廣泛的推理能力，但在專業領域如旅遊中，準確性和可靠性卻存在困難，因為推理依賴於精確的定義、規則和專家定義的概念框架，而自信但無根據的輸出則源於模型未能內化基礎領域圖的推理失敗，而不僅僅是缺乏領域知識。我們提出了一個模組化的管道，用於構建基於專家設計的知識圖（KG）的旅遊領域推理LLM。我們的管道整合了一個編碼領域實體及其關係的旅遊KG、一個自下而上的構建程序，該程序遍歷KG以生成多跳問題回答（QA）對、一個監督微調階段，該階段使用生成的QA對作為可審計的推理痕跡，將領域知識嵌入到具有推理能力的LLM中，以及一個旅遊領域基準數據集，用於測量微調模型的準確性和標定。我們使用帶有LoRA適應的Qwen3-4B評估我們的方法。我們的推理模型在基準測試中達到了$82.4\%$的精確匹配。這一表現顯著超過了預訓練的Qwen3-4B基準的$22.4\%$。標定分析將剩餘的$17.57\%$錯誤分解為兩種不同的失敗模式：一種是過於自信的多標籤解碼器，在大多數雙答案錯誤中預測正確答案加上一個虛假的選項，另一種是在單答案問題上的較小推理失敗，儘管支持事實存在於KG中，但模型未能重建正確的多跳路徑。這一分裂確認了明確的KG基礎推理顯著提高了LLMs在專業領域中的準確性和不確定性解釋，並將每個選項的標定和考慮追蹤長度的解碼隔離為下一個改進的方向。
2606.29254.author: Vignesh Ram Nithin Kappagantula et.al.
2606.29254.authors: Vignesh Ram Nithin Kappagantula, Shayan Hassantabar, Samuel Simpson, Golnaz Moallem
